content: read reviews/726.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/726.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/726.md.

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 726

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ¬ß1‚Äď7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  ```
  /-
  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
  ...
  -/
  ```
- [x] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).

> Exact standard copyright block present at lines 1‚Äď15. No leading blank line. Year is 2026.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  ```
  import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
  ```
- [x] No other imports are present.

> Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`. Single blank line separates it from the copyright block. No other imports.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 726`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source:
  ```
  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/726](https://www.erdosproblems.com/726)
  ```
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.

> Lines 19‚Äď27. Heading is `# ErdŇĎs Problem 726`. Reference link is correct. Brief description: "A conjecture of ErdŇĎs, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus." Bibliography entry: `[EGRS75] ErdŇĎs, P., Graham, R., Ruzsa, I., and Straus, E.`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos726 ... end Erdos726`).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos726`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces.

> Namespace `Erdos726` at lines 33‚Äď57. `open Filter Nat` (line 29) and `open scoped Topology Real` (line 31) both precede the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`.
- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.

> `erdos726Sum` (lines 35‚Äď40): has a docstring explaining the weighted sum, marked `noncomputable`, no `sorry`, uses `lowerCamelCase` (returns `‚ĄĚ`). No local notation needed ‚ÄĒ the definition is sufficiently clear without it. No sanity-check tests provided (optional, but could be beneficial to verify the function on small examples).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_726` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s).

> `theorem erdos_726` at line 52. Attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`. Docstring (lines 42‚Äď50) includes LaTeX-style mathematical statement and references `[EGRS75]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion: the sum is asymptotically equal to (log log n)/2. This is **Case 3** ‚ÄĒ a direct assertion, not a yes/no question or a "find the value" problem.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper).

> Correct. The theorem directly asserts `Tendsto (fun n => erdos726Sum n / (Real.log (Real.log ‚ÜĎn) / 2)) atTop (nhds 1)`, which is the standard formulation of f(n) ~ g(n) as f/g ‚Üí 1.

**Common rules for all cases with `answer()`:**

- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

> N/A ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` used, no quantified theorem parameters. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] The value is one of the valid categories: `research open`.
- [x] A `research open` theorem must contain `sorry` in its proof body.

> `@[category research open]` on line 51. The problem is confirmed open per erdosproblems.com. Proof body contains `sorry` (line 55).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem`, `lemma`, and `example` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Every number is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

> `AMS 11` on line 51. Single code, number theory (11) is appropriate for a problem about prime distributions. 11 is a valid MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (not bare `sorry` or `by { sorry }`).
- [x] Multi-line proofs with `sorry` are acceptable when intermediate structure is needed.
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No `sorry` in any file under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] Do **not** include informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] Avoid `native_decide`.

> Proof body is `:= by\n  sorry` (lines 54‚Äď55). This is the multi-line format, acceptable because the theorem statement spans multiple lines. No sorry in the `erdos726Sum` definition. No proof sketches. No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present.

> The problem is a single asymptotic conjecture with no sub-parts or variant formulations needed.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (aim for <100 characters; break long lines).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

> Indentation is consistently 2 spaces. Unicode `‚Ąē` and `‚ĄĚ` used. No debug code or linter-disabling options. Longest line is ~79 characters (line 39). File is clean and well-formatted.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors** (warnings from `sorry` in open problems are expected and acceptable).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass:
  - `linter.style.copyright`
  - `linter.style.category_attribute`
  - `linter.style.ams_attribute`
  - `linter.style.answer_attribute`
  - `linter.style.namespace`

> **Not verified** ‚ÄĒ build not run during this review. Must be confirmed before merge.

---

## Mathematical Fidelity

The original problem (erdosproblems.com/726) conjectures that as n ‚Üí ‚ąě:

$$\sum_{\substack{p \le n \\ p \text{ prime}}} \mathbf{1}_{n \bmod p \in (p/2, p)} \cdot \frac{1}{p} \sim \frac{\log \log n}{2}$$

The formalization captures this correctly:

1. **`erdos726Sum`** sums `1/p` over primes `p ‚Č§ n` where `2 * (n % p) > p`, which is equivalent to `n mod p ‚ąą (p/2, p)` since `n % p ‚ąą {0, ..., p-1}` and `2r > p` ‚üļ `r > p/2` in naturals. The upper bound `r < p` is automatic for modular remainders.

2. **`erdos_726`** asserts `Tendsto (fun n => erdos726Sum n / (log(log n) / 2)) atTop (nhds 1)`, which is the standard formulation of `f ~ g` as `f/g ‚Üí 1`.

**Verdict: Faithful formalization.**

---

## Summary

| Section | Status |
|---|---|
| 1. Copyright Header | PASS |
| 2. Import Statement | PASS |
| 3. Module Docstring | PASS |
| 4. Namespace | PASS |
| 5. Custom Definitions | PASS |
| 6. Theorem Statements | PASS |
| 7. Category Attribute | PASS |
| 8. AMS Attribute | PASS |
| 9. Proof Bodies | PASS |
| 10. Variants | N/A |
| 11. Code Quality | PASS |
| 12. Build Verification | **NOT VERIFIED** |

**Overall: PASS** (pending build verification). Clean, well-structured formalization with no issues found.

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 726
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/726](https://www.erdosproblems.com/726)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA conjecture of ErdŇĎs, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[EGRS75] ErdŇĎs, P., Graham, R., Ruzsa, I., and Straus, E.
    27‚Üí-/
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üíopen Filter Nat
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos726
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- The weighted sum of $1/p$ over primes $p \le n$ for which $n \bmod p$ lies in $(p/2, p)$,
    36‚Üíi.e., $2 \cdot (n \bmod p) > p$. This captures the condition $n \equiv r \pmod{p}$ for some
    37‚Üíinteger $r$ with $p/2 < r < p$. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def erdos726Sum (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    39‚Üí  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter Nat.Prime).sum
    40‚Üí    (fun p => if 2 * (n % p) > p then (1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (p : ‚ĄĚ) else 0)
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/--
    43‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 726 [EGRS75]:
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíAs $n \to \infty$,
    46‚Üí$$\sum_{\substack{p \le n \\ p \text{ prime}}} \mathbf{1}_{n \in (p/2, p) \pmod{p}} \cdot
    47‚Üí\frac{1}{p} \sim \frac{\log \log n}{2}.$$
    48‚Üí
    49‚ÜíStated as: the ratio of the sum to $(\log \log n)/2$ tends to $1$.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    52‚Üítheorem erdos_726 :
    53‚Üí    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => erdos726Sum n / (Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) / 2))
    54‚Üí      atTop (nhds 1) := by
    55‚Üí  sorry
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üíend Erdos726
    58‚Üí

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 726

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/726](https://www.erdosproblems.com/726)

A conjecture of ErdŇĎs, Graham, Ruzsa, and Straus.

[EGRS75] ErdŇĎs, P., Graham, R., Ruzsa, I., and Straus, E.
-/

open Filter Nat

open scoped Topology Real

namespace Erdos726

/-- The weighted sum of $1/p$ over primes $p \le n$ for which $n \bmod p$ lies in $(p/2, p)$,
i.e., $2 \cdot (n \bmod p) > p$. This captures the condition $n \equiv r \pmod{p}$ for some
integer $r$ with $p/2 < r < p$. -/
noncomputable def erdos726Sum (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter Nat.Prime).sum
    (fun p => if 2 * (n % p) > p then (1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (p : ‚ĄĚ) else 0)

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 726 [EGRS75]:

As $n \to \infty$,
$$\sum_{\substack{p \le n \\ p \text{ prime}}} \mathbf{1}_{n \in (p/2, p) \pmod{p}} \cdot
\frac{1}{p} \sim \frac{\log \log n}{2}.$$

Stated as: the ratio of the sum to $(\log \log n)/2$ tends to $1$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_726 :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => erdos726Sum n / (Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) / 2))
      atTop (nhds 1) := by
  sorry

end Erdos726
